I've been slowly fighting my way through the issues compiling CuPy 13.3.0 on an AMD cluster I have access to with ROCm 6.1.1 (it's not my cluster).

There are a bunch of deprecations I need to report elsewhere  from ROCm 6.x but the first problem is that if I install the latest setuptools, build fail to detect hipblas properly.

The default setuptools on the cluster is 65.5.0 which works, but if I update to the latest from pip (74.1.1 at time of writing) compiling fails early on with it unable to find files that are... patently there (e.g. `hipblas.h`) and on my includes:

```
Generating cache key from header files...
Cache key (1610 files matching /dev/shm/okenway/cupy/tmp.ZGQsgqHQ64/cupy/cupy/_core/include/**): aacd7ccf6b32f54c63d4eb742fc1188ea94d1b77

-------- Configuring Module: cuda --------
/home/okenway/tmp/tmpgr7uvb35/a.cpp:3:10: fatal error: hipblas.h: No such file or directory
    3 | #include <hipblas.h>
      |          ^~~~~~~~~~~
compilation terminated.
command '/home/software/gcc/14.1.0/bin/g++' failed with exit code 1

************************************************************
* CuPy Configuration Summary                               *
************************************************************

Build Environment:
  Include directories: ['/opt/rocm/include/hipcub', '/dev/shm/okenway/cupy/tmp.ZGQsgqHQ64/cupy/cupy/_core/include', '/opt/rocm/include', '/opt/rocm/include/hip', '/opt/rocm/include/rocrand', '/opt/rocm/include/hiprand', '/opt/rocm/include/roctracer']
  Library directories: ['/opt/rocm/lib']
  nvcc command       : (not found)
  hipcc command      : ['/opt/rocm/bin/hipcc']

Environment Variables:
  CFLAGS          : -I/opt/rocm/include/hipblas -I/opt/rocm/include/hipsparse -I/opt/rocm/include/hipfft -I/opt/rocm/include/rocsolver -I/opt/rocm/include/rccl
  LDFLAGS         : (none)
  LIBRARY_PATH    : /home/software/python/3.11.7/lib:/cm/shared/apps/slurm/current/lib64/slurm:/cm/shared/apps/slurm/current/lib64
  CUDA_PATH       : (none)
  NVCC            : (none)
  HIPCC           : (none)
  ROCM_HOME       : /opt/rocm

Modules:
  cuda      : No
    -> Include files not found: ['hip/hip_runtime_api.h', 'hip/hiprtc.h', 'hipblas.h', 'hiprand/hiprand.h', 'hipsparse.h', 'hipfft.h', 'roctx.h', 'rocsolver.h']
    -> Check your CFLAGS environment variable.

ERROR: CUDA could not be found on your system.

HINT: You are trying to build CuPy from source, which is NOT recommended for general use.
      Please consider using binary packages instead.

Please refer to the Installation Guide for details:
https://docs.cupy.dev/en/stable/install.html

************************************************************

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/dev/shm/okenway/cupy/tmp.ZGQsgqHQ64/cupy/setup.py", line 95, in <module>
    ext_modules = cupy_setup_build.get_ext_modules(True, ctx)
                  ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/dev/shm/okenway/cupy/tmp.ZGQsgqHQ64/cupy/install/cupy_builder/cupy_setup_build.py", line 514, in get_ext_modules
    extensions = make_extensions(ctx, compiler, use_cython)
                 ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/dev/shm/okenway/cupy/tmp.ZGQsgqHQ64/cupy/install/cupy_builder/cupy_setup_build.py", line 363, in make_extensions
    raise Exception('Your CUDA environment is invalid. '
Exception: Your CUDA environment is invalid. Please check above error log.
```

### To Reproduce

1. Create a new virtualenvironment on a system with ROCm 6.x
2. Install latest setuptools (74.1.1) from pip
3. Set environment variables.

```
export ROCM_HOME=/opt/rocm
export HCC_AMDGPU_TARGET=gfx90a
export CFLAGS="-I/opt/rocm/include/hipblas -I/opt/rocm/include/hipsparse -I/opt/rocm/include/hipfft -I/opt/rocm/include/rocsolver -I/opt/rocm/include/rccl"
```

4. Either `pip install cupy` or clone the source and run `python3 setup.py bdist_wheel`



### Installation

None

### Environment

I've been working on getting CuPy to compile on a cluster with AMD GPUs which has ROCm 6.1.1 (see https://github.com/cupy/cupy/issues/8585 for related issue) and there are a number of places in the code for CuPy where for HIP devices it refers to things are were previously deprecated and are now removed in 6.x

Specifically:

`hipMemoryTypeDevice` and `hipMemoryTypeHost` no longer have an attribute `memoryType` but follow Cuda in having `type` instead. (https://github.com/owainkenwayucl/boyd/blob/master/billets/cupy/patches/cupy_hip_runtime.h.patch + https://github.com/owainkenwayucl/boyd/blob/master/billets/cupy/patches/_runtime_typedef.pxi.patch, https://github.com/owainkenwayucl/boyd/blob/master/billets/cupy/patches/runtime.pyx.patch)

`gcnArch` has now also been removed (https://github.com/owainkenwayucl/boyd/blob/master/billets/cupy/patches/runtime.pyx.patch)

My patches are messy so someone should probably take a proper look at this and fix it for future releases.


### To Reproduce

Compile CuPy 13.3.0 on ROCm 6.1.1

### Installation

None

### Environment

N/A

Description

I've been working on getting CuPy to compile on a cluster with AMD GPUs which has ROCm 6.1.1 (see https://github.com/cupy/cupy/issues/8585 for related issue) and there are a number of places in the code for CuPy where for HIP devices it refers to things are were previously deprecated and are now removed in 6.x

Specifically:

`hipMemoryTypeDevice` and `hipMemoryTypeHost` no longer have an attribute `memoryType` but follow Cuda in having `type` instead. (https://github.com/owainkenwayucl/boyd/blob/master/billets/cupy/patches/cupy_hip_runtime.h.patch + https://github.com/owainkenwayucl/boyd/blob/master/billets/cupy/patches/_runtime_typedef.pxi.patch, https://github.com/owainkenwayucl/boyd/blob/master/billets/cupy/patches/runtime.pyx.patch)

`gcnArch` has now also been removed (https://github.com/owainkenwayucl/boyd/blob/master/billets/cupy/patches/runtime.pyx.patch)

My patches are messy so someone should probably take a proper look at this and fix it for future releases.

ub.com/users/kmaehashi/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmaehashi/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"assignees":[{"login":"kmaehashi","id":939877,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjkzOTg3Nw==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/939877?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmaehashi","html_url":"https://github.com/kmaehashi","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmaehashi/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmaehashi/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmaehashi/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmaehashi/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmaehashi/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmaehashi/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmaehashi/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmaehashi/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmaehashi/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false}],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2024-09-04T16:56:21Z","updated_at":"2024-09-05T09:21:53Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Description

I've been working on getting CuPy to compile on a cluster with AMD GPUs which has ROCm 6.1.1 (see https://github.com/cupy/cupy/issues/8585 for related issue) and there are a number of places in the code for CuPy where for HIP devices it refers to things are were previously deprecated and are now removed in 6.x

Specifically:

`hipMemoryTypeDevice` and `hipMemoryTypeHost` no longer have an attribute `memoryType` but follow Cuda in having `type` instead. thub.com/users/kmaehashi/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmaehashi/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"assignees":[{"login":"kmaehashi","id":939877,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjkzOTg3Nw==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/939877?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmaehashi","html_url":"https://github.com/kmaehashi","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmaehashi/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmaehashi/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmaehashi/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmaehashi/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmaehashi/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmaehashi/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmaehashi/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmaehashi/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/kmaehashi/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false}],"milestone":null,"comments":5,"created_at":"2024-09-04T16:56:21Z","updated_at":"2024-09-05T09:41:35Z","closed_at":"2024-09-05T09:41:34Z","author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Description

I've been working on getting CuPy to compile on a cluster with AMD GPUs which has ROCm 6.1.1 (see https://github.com/cupy/cupy/issues/8585 for related issue) and there are a number of places in the code for CuPy where for HIP devices it refers to things are were previously deprecated and are now removed in 6.x

Specifically:

`hipMemoryTypeDevice` and `hipMemoryTypeHost` no longer have an attribute `memoryType` but follow Cuda in having `type` instead. (https://github.com/owainkenwayucl/boyd/blob/master/billets/cupy/patches/cupy_hip_runtime.h.patch + https://github.com/owainkenwayucl/boyd/blob/master/billets/cupy/patches/_runtime_typedef.pxi.patch, https://github.com/owainkenwayucl/boyd/blob/master/billets/cupy/patches/runtime.pyx.patch)

`gcnArch` has now also been removed (https://github.com/owainkenwayucl/boyd/blob/master/billets/cupy/patches/runtime.pyx.patch)

My patches are messy so someone should probably take a proper look at this and fix it for future releases.


### To Reproduce

Compile CuPy 13.3.0 on ROCm 6.1.1

### Installation

None

### Environment

N/A

### Additional Information

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3379","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3379/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3379/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3379/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3379","id":1589253476,"node_id":"I_kwDOBOhtSc5euhVk","number":3379,"title":"Small  documentation fix for Pkg.jl documentation to aid understanding of new users.","user":{"login":"owainkenwayucl","id":4256093,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNTYwOTM=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/4256093?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/owainkenwayucl","html_url":"https://github.com/owainkenwayucl","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/owainkenwayucl/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/owainkenwayucl/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/owainkenwayucl/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/owainkenwayucl/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/owainkenwayucl/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/owainkenwayucl/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/owainkenwayucl/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/owainkenwayucl/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/owainkenwayucl/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-02-17T12:02:48Z","updated_at":"2023-02-17T12:02:48Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"<!--
If you have a question please search or post to our Discourse site: https://discourse.julialang.org.
We use the GitHub issue tracker for bug reports and feature requests only.

If you're submitting a bug report, be sure to include as much relevant information as
possible, including:

1. The output of `versioninfo()`
2. How you installed Julia
3. A minimal working example (MWE), also known as a minimum reproducible example

If you're experiencing a problem with a particular package, open an issue on that
package's repository instead.

Thanks for contributing to the Julia project!
-->

I've been struggling for a while with how to do "multiple file projects" in the way I do in Python/Fortran/whatever in Julia - i.e. by having local packages - and due to some experimentation I think I've worked out what the problem is.

In the `Pkg.jl` docmentation on how to create a package (https://github.com/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/blob/master/docs/src/creating-packages.md) there is an important step which looks like a "not important" step, namely:


```
julia> cd("HelloWorld")

shell> tree .
.
├── Project.toml
└── src
    └── HelloWorld.jl

1 directory, 2 files
```

To a naive reader of the documentation, this looks like a box they can skip over, because it's just showing the layout of the file-system, however as you all (and now I!) know it's actually important to execute the `cd` command or else nothing that follows will work.

My suggestion would be to make it clear that the `cd` is an important step by having a separate box for it, distinct from the `tree .` command and making clear its necessity.
